data.title: Claude omitted PIL heading when creating epic 736d-b957 manually post-approval
data.description:
## Summary

When manually creating epic 736d-b957 via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket create epic` after an approved brainstorm spec, I wrote a custom description (Problem / Solution / SCs / Validation Evidence / Sibling Epics / Related / Open Items) but omitted the `### Planning Intelligence Log` heading required by brainstorm Phase 3 Step 1 template. Consequences:

1. The existing tag-migration script (`ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh`) keys off the PIL heading to identify scrutiny-complete epics. Without it, 736d-b957 was invisible to the retroactive cleanup path.
2. The epic was tagged `scrutiny:pending` at create, which would have blocked `/dso:preplanning` and `/dso:implementation-plan` entry — exactly the meta-pattern the epic itself exists to prevent.

## Rationalization (why I did it)

I treated the Phase 3 Step 1 description template as guidance for ticket creation from inside an active brainstorm flow, not as a strict requirement when executing `ticket create epic` manually in response to an approved spec presentation. Specifically:

1. **Template drift from approval context**: The approval gate (Phase 2 Step 4) presented the spec as Context + SCs + Dependencies. I mentally formed "description = the approved spec" rather than "description = Phase 3 Step 1 template which wraps the approved spec plus a PIL log generated at approval time."
2. **Information-density bias**: I prioritized dense, searchable content (numbered SCs, evidence summary, related tickets) over structural compliance with the PIL heading marker. I did not recognize that the heading itself is a contract signal consumed by downstream tooling, not a decorative structure.
3. **Session compaction masked the template**: The prior session was summarized before ticket creation; the summary preserved the spec but not the brainstorm skill text with the Phase 3 Step 1 template. I did not re-read `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` before running `ticket create`, so I did not refresh the template requirement into context.
4. **Ironic meta-pattern instance**: The epic (736d-b957) exists because "downstream stages trust upstream outputs without validating implicit assumptions." My failure is an instance of exactly that: Phase 3 trusted the Phase 2 spec as the ticket description without validating that all Phase 3-required structural markers (PIL) were constructed from approval-time state.

## Root Cause Category

LLM behavioral — instruction-drift under compaction. Mitigations landing in epic 736d-b957 (stage-boundary PRECONDITIONS manifest) would catch this class of failure at the ticket-create boundary by treating the template as a structural contract with validator enforcement.

## Remedy Applied

PIL section retro-injected into 736d-b957 description; tags updated (`scrutiny:pending` removed, `brainstorm:complete` added) to match the actual scrutiny-complete state.

## Proposed Prevention

1. Make the Phase 3 Step 1 template a loaded-inline reference in any brainstorm continuation session (re-read the skill file before `ticket create`).
2. Consider a pre-create validator that rejects epic descriptions lacking the PIL heading when `scrutiny:pending` is not explicitly requested (this is a natural candidate for the stage-boundary PRECONDITIONS manifest itself).
